attractions.name: Senso-ji Temple
attractions.description: Tokyo's oldest and most famous Buddhist temple in Asakusa, approached via the Kaminarimon 'Thunder Gate' and a bustling shopping street, Nakamise-dori.

attractions.name: Shibuya Crossing
attractions.description: The world-famous scramble crossing outside Shibuya Station, where hundreds cross in every direction at once — best viewed from a nearby cafe or the Shibuya Sky observation deck.

attractions.name: Meiji Shrine
attractions.description: A serene Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji, set within a forested park near Harajuku — a peaceful contrast to the surrounding city.

attractions.name: Tokyo Skytree
attractions.description: One of the world's tallest towers, with two observation decks offering panoramic views across the Kanto region on clear days.
attractions.transport_tips: Tokyo Skytree Station or Oshiage Station

attractions.name: Akihabara
attractions.description: Tokyo's electronics and otaku-culture district, packed with multi-storey electronics stores, anime and manga shops, and retro arcades.

attractions.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
attractions.description: The bustling street-food and retail market that remains at Tsukiji after the wholesale fish auctions moved to Toyosu — famous for fresh sushi breakfasts and street snacks.

attractions.name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
attractions.description: A large landscaped park blending Japanese, French, and English garden styles — a favourite cherry blossom viewing spot in spring.

attractions.name: teamLab Digital Art Museum
attractions.description: An immersive digital art experience of large-scale, interactive light and projection installations — hugely popular, advance timed tickets recommended.
attractions.transport_tips: Check current venue location and book tickets online in advance

attractions.name: Harajuku Takeshita Street
attractions.description: A colourful, crowded street famous for youth fashion, crepe stands, and quirky boutique shops.

restaurants.name: Ichiran Ramen (chain, multiple branches)
restaurants.description: A famous tonkotsu ramen chain known for individual booth seating and a rich, customizable pork-broth ramen.

restaurants.name: Omoide Yokocho, Shinjuku
restaurants.description: A nostalgic, lantern-lit alley of tiny yakitori and izakaya stalls tucked beside Shinjuku Station, dating back to the post-war era.

restaurants.name: Depachika Food Halls (department store basements)
restaurants.description: Basement-level gourmet food halls in major department stores like Isetan or Takashimaya, offering high-quality bento, sweets, and prepared dishes.

hidden_gems.name: Yanaka Ginza
hidden_gems.description: An old-Tokyo shopping street in the Yanaka district that survived WWII bombing, retaining a nostalgic, low-rise Showa-era atmosphere far from the tourist crowds.

hidden_gems.name: Nezu Shrine
hidden_gems.description: A beautiful, lesser-visited shrine known for its tunnel of vermilion torii gates and azalea garden — often compared to Kyoto's Fushimi Inari at a fraction of the crowd.

nightlife.name: Golden Gai, Shinjuku
nightlife.description: A warren of over 200 tiny, atmospheric bars packed into narrow alleys, each seating only a handful of people — a quintessential Tokyo nightlife experience.
nightlife.budget_category: Mid-range (some bars charge a seating fee)

nightlife.name: Roppongi
nightlife.description: Tokyo's international nightlife hub with clubs, lounge bars, and the Mori Art Museum/Tokyo City View observation deck.

shopping.name: Ginza
shopping.description: Tokyo's upscale shopping district with flagship luxury stores, department stores, and elegant cafes.

festivals.name: Cherry Blossom (Hanami) Season
festivals.description: Parks across Tokyo (Ueno, Shinjuku Gyoen, Meguro River) fill with picnickers under blooming sakura trees.
festivals.best_time: Late March to early April (dates shift yearly with bloom forecasts)

festivals.name: Sanja Matsuri
festivals.description: One of Tokyo's largest and liveliest Shinto festivals, held around Asakusa Shrine with mikoshi (portable shrine) processions.

adventure_activities.name: Mount Takao Day Hike
adventure_activities.description: A popular, accessible mountain hike (with cable car option) about an hour from central Tokyo, with temple stops and city views from the summit.

local_tips: Get a Suica or Pasmo IC card immediately on arrival for seamless travel on trains, buses, and even convenience store payments.
Most restaurants and vending machines still favour cash, though card/IC payment acceptance has grown significantly.
Trains stop running around midnight to 1am — plan late nights around the last-train time or budget for a taxi.
Tipping is not customary in Japan and can even cause confusion — service charges, if any, are included in the bill.

safety.general: Tokyo is among the safest major cities in the world for tourists, including solo and female travellers, with very low street crime.
safety.natural_hazards: Japan is earthquake-prone; familiarize yourself with hotel emergency exits and the NHK World app for alerts.
safety.emergency_language: English signage is common in central Tokyo and on trains, but less so in smaller local eateries.

culture.overview: Tokyo blends centuries-old Shinto/Buddhist tradition with cutting-edge pop culture and technology, and places a strong cultural emphasis on politeness, punctuality, and public order.
culture.etiquette: Avoid eating while walking in crowded areas, don't speak loudly on trains, and bow lightly as a greeting; remove shoes when a genkan (entryway) is present.

transport.getting_around: An extensive train and subway network (JR + Tokyo Metro + Toei) covers almost everywhere; a Suica/Pasmo IC card works across all of them.
transport.airport: Narita and Haneda airports; Haneda is closer to central Tokyo via the Tokyo Monorail or Keikyu line.

visa_information.indian_passport_holders: Indian citizens generally need a visa for Japan, though eligible frequent travellers can apply for a multiple-entry visa; check current requirements before booking.
visa_information.general: Many other nationalities (US, EU, UK, etc.) enjoy visa-free short-stay entry — verify based on passport.

weather.best_time_to_visit: March-May (spring, cherry blossoms) and September-November (autumn foliage)
weather.avoid: Mid-June to July (rainy season/tsuyu) and peak summer humidity in August
